title: Water Inlet Error
shortDescription: The washer is unable to fill with water within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates that the washing machine has failed to detect adequate water inflow within the programmed time limit. This typically points to a restricted or closed water supply, a faulty inlet valve, or a malfunctioning water level sensor. The cycle will pause until the issue is resolved.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is turned off or has low pressure
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Inlet hose is kinked, pinched, or blocked by sediment
Water level pressure sensor or hose is faulty
Inlet filter screen is clogged with debris

symptoms: E1 error displayed on the control panel
Washer does not fill with water
Very slow water fill
Cycle pauses shortly after starting
Buzzing sound from inlet valve area with no water flow

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify that the water supply tap behind or beside the washer is fully open. Run the tap separately to confirm adequate water pressure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose and filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect the inlet hose from the back of the machine. Clean the mesh filter screen at the valve inlet. Check the hose for kinks or blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the multimeter set to resistance mode, disconnect the valve wiring and measure across the solenoid coil terminals. A reading of 500-1500 ohms is normal. No reading or very high resistance indicates a failed valve.

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure sensor hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose connected to the water level pressure sensor (usually near the top of the tub). Ensure it is not kinked, cracked, or disconnected. Blow gently through the hose to check for blockages.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve or pressure sensor needs replacement and you are not comfortable working with electrical connections.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E1 water inlet error fix

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E1?
faq.a: Yes. If the mains water pressure is below approximately 0.3 bar, the washer may not fill quickly enough and will trigger E1. Check with your water utility if pressure is consistently low.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet filter?
faq.a: Turn off the tap, unscrew the inlet hose from the back of the machine, and carefully pull out the small mesh screen with pliers. Rinse under running water and reinstall.

faq.q: Will E1 clear on its own?
faq.a: E1 will clear once the underlying water supply issue is resolved and the washer is restarted or reset by unplugging for a few minutes.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water from the drum within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E2 signals that the washer has been unable to pump out water from the tub within the programmed timeout. This is commonly caused by a blocked drain filter, a kinked drain hose, or a faulty drain pump. Water will remain inside the drum.

causes.cause: Drain filter is clogged with lint, coins, or debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed by a foreign object
Drain pump motor has failed electrically

symptoms: E2 error displayed on the control panel
Standing water remains in the drum
Clothes are dripping wet after the cycle
Drain pump is silent when it should be running
Gurgling or humming sounds from the pump area

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access panel at the bottom front of the washer. Place towels and a shallow dish underneath. Slowly unscrew the drain filter cap and allow trapped water to flow out. Remove any debris, coins, or lint from the filter and housing.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer away from the wall and check the drain hose for kinks, pinches, or obstructions. Disconnect the hose from the standpipe or sink and blow through it to verify it is clear.

fixSteps.title: Check the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the drain filter removed, shine a flashlight into the pump housing. You should see the impeller blades. Try to rotate them with your finger — they should spin freely. Remove any foreign objects jamming the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump from the bottom or rear of the machine. Disconnect the wiring and test resistance across the pump motor terminals with a multimeter. A normal reading is 5-30 ohms. If open circuit, the pump needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and requires replacement, or if the blockage is deep inside the machine plumbing.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E2 drain error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Toshiba recommends cleaning the drain filter every 1-2 months, or more frequently if you wash items that shed a lot of lint or fibers.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the water?
faq.a: Yes. Place a shallow container under the drain filter access panel and slowly unscrew the filter cap. The trapped water will flow out by gravity.

faq.q: What if the pump runs but water does not drain?
faq.a: The impeller inside the pump may be broken or the drain hose may have an internal blockage. Inspect both components carefully.

title: Overflow or Overfill Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected that the water level inside the drum has exceeded the safe limit.
fullDescription: Error E3 indicates that the water level sensor has detected water above the maximum safe level inside the tub. This can be caused by a faulty water inlet valve that does not close properly, a defective pressure sensor, or a siphoning issue with the drain hose installation.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open or leaking internally
Water level pressure sensor is faulty and reading incorrectly
Pressure sensor hose is disconnected or has a hole
Drain hose installed too low, causing siphoning from the sink or standpipe

symptoms: E3 error displayed on the control panel
Water visibly overflowing from the detergent drawer or door seal
Washer fills continuously and does not stop
Water leaking from underneath the machine
Cycle stops abruptly during the fill phase

fixSteps.title: Power off and close water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the washing machine and close the water supply tap to prevent further flooding. Mop up any spilled water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose installation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drain hose is installed at a height of 60-90 cm above floor level where it enters the standpipe or sink. If it is too low, water from the sink or standpipe can siphon back into the machine.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure sensor hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose connecting the tub to the pressure switch. Check that it is firmly connected at both ends and has no holes or cracks. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer in an off state and the water supply turned on, observe whether water continues to trickle into the tub. If it does, the inlet valve is leaking internally and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot determine the cause of the overflow or if the inlet valve needs replacement and you are not comfortable with the repair.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E3 overflow error fix

faq.q: Is E3 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be if water is overflowing onto the floor. Turn off the machine and water supply immediately to prevent water damage.

faq.q: What is siphoning?
faq.a: Siphoning occurs when dirty water from the drain flows back into the washer because the drain hose is installed too low, creating a vacuum effect.

faq.q: Can a faulty detergent drawer cause overflow?
faq.a: A cracked or misaligned detergent drawer can cause water to leak out but would not typically trigger E3, which relates to the internal water level sensor.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle.
fullDescription: Error E4 indicates the washer cannot achieve a safe spin speed because the laundry inside the drum is unevenly distributed. The machine will repeatedly attempt to redistribute the load by reversing and pausing, but if balance cannot be achieved, it will stop and display E4.

causes.cause: Laundry is bunched up on one side of the drum
Single heavy item such as a towel or blanket is causing imbalance
Drum shock absorbers are worn and cannot dampen vibration
Suspension springs are broken or detached

symptoms: E4 error displayed on the control panel
Excessive vibration or shaking during spin
Loud banging or thumping noises
Washer moves or walks across the floor
Spin cycle fails to complete

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and rearrange the laundry so it is evenly distributed around the drum. Avoid washing a single large item alone; add a few towels to help balance.

fixSteps.title: Check machine leveling
fixSteps.instruction: Place a spirit level on top of the washer. Adjust the four leveling feet until the machine is perfectly level and all feet are firmly on the floor. Lock the feet with the lock nuts.

fixSteps.title: Inspect shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the washer back or remove the rear panel to access the shock absorbers. Push down on the drum firmly and release — it should return to position with minimal bouncing. If it bounces repeatedly, the shock absorbers are worn.

fixSteps.title: Check suspension springs
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the springs that support the drum from the top of the cabinet. All springs should be intact and firmly hooked at both ends. Replace any stretched or broken springs.

whenToCallPro: If the shock absorbers or springs need replacement and you are not comfortable disassembling the machine.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E4 unbalanced load error fix

faq.q: Can I override E4 and force a spin?
faq.a: No. The unbalance protection is a safety feature to prevent damage to the machine and surrounding structures. Redistribute the load instead.

faq.q: Why does E4 happen with small loads?
faq.a: Small loads are more prone to bunching on one side. Adding a couple of towels to small loads helps maintain balance during spinning.

faq.q: How long do shock absorbers last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years depending on usage. Heavy use and frequent high-spin cycles accelerate wear.

title: Motor or Drive Error
shortDescription: The washer motor is not operating correctly or the drum is unable to rotate.
fullDescription: Error E5 indicates the control board cannot drive the motor at the required speed, or the motor is drawing excessive current. This may be due to a jammed drum, a faulty motor, a broken drive belt, or a control board issue.

causes.cause: Drive belt is broken or has slipped off the pulley
Motor brushes are worn (on brush-type motors)
Foreign object is jamming the drum
Motor winding has an open or short circuit

symptoms: E5 error displayed on the control panel
Drum does not spin or agitate
Motor hums but drum does not rotate
Burning smell from the motor area
Unusual grinding or scraping noise

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, then plug it back in and attempt a new cycle. If E5 recurs, proceed with further diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate the drum by hand. It should turn smoothly with some resistance from the belt. If it is completely jammed or makes grinding noises, a foreign object may be lodged between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and check the belt running between the motor pulley and drum pulley. If the belt is broken, worn, or loose, replace it. Install the new belt on the motor pulley first, then stretch it onto the drum pulley while turning the drum.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring and measure resistance across the motor winding terminals. Typical readings are 2-10 ohms. Also check for short to ground between each terminal and the motor body. If readings are abnormal, the motor must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the motor itself has failed or if you suspect the main control board is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E5 motor drive error fix

faq.q: How can I tell if the belt is broken?
faq.a: If the drum spins freely by hand with almost no resistance, the belt is likely broken or has come off the pulleys. A working belt provides moderate resistance.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E5?
faq.a: Yes. A power surge can damage the motor control circuitry on the main board. A simple reset may resolve the issue if no permanent damage occurred.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the motor on an old machine?
faq.a: If the machine is more than 8-10 years old, compare the motor replacement cost against a new washer. Motors typically cost 30-50 percent of a new machine.

title: Heating Element Error
shortDescription: The water heating element is not functioning properly or the water is not reaching the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error E6 indicates the washer's heating element has failed to raise the water temperature to the programmed level within the allowed time. This can be caused by a burned-out heater, a faulty NTC temperature sensor, or wiring issues.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or has an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is faulty and sending incorrect readings
Wiring between the heater and control board is damaged
Limescale buildup on the heating element reducing efficiency

symptoms: E6 error displayed on the control panel
Water remains cold during hot wash cycles
Wash cycle takes much longer than usual
Clothes not cleaned properly on warm or hot settings
Cycle stops before completing

fixSteps.title: Run a cold wash test
fixSteps.instruction: Select a cold wash cycle. If it completes without error, the issue is confirmed as heater-related. If E6 appears even on cold wash, the temperature sensor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater from the rear or bottom of the machine. Disconnect the wires and measure resistance across the heater terminals. A normal reading is 20-40 ohms. Also check for continuity to the heater body (ground fault) — there should be no continuity.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC temperature sensor (usually embedded in or near the heater). Disconnect it and measure resistance. At room temperature (20°C) it should read approximately 4.7-6 kilo-ohms. Significantly different values indicate a faulty sensor.

fixSteps.title: Descale the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater tests good but is coated in limescale, run an empty hot wash cycle with 200 ml of white vinegar or a commercial descaler to dissolve the buildup.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element needs replacement and you are not confident working with high-current electrical components.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E6 heating element error fix

faq.q: Can I use the washer with cold water only?
faq.a: You can run cold wash cycles to continue using the washer temporarily, but hot and warm washes will not work properly until the heater issue is resolved.

faq.q: How long does a heating element last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years. Hard water areas with high limescale content will shorten the lifespan significantly.

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. It is a thermistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases, allowing the control board to monitor water temperature.

title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is not providing valid readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error E7 indicates the control board is receiving erratic or no signal from the water level pressure sensor. This sensor monitors how much water is in the drum, and without it the machine cannot safely fill or drain.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor hose is disconnected, kinked, or cracked
Pressure switch or electronic pressure sensor has failed
Wiring harness between sensor and control board is damaged
Air trap chamber at the bottom of the tub is blocked with detergent residue

symptoms: E7 error displayed on the control panel
Washer overfills or underfills with water
Machine does not start the wash cycle
Cycle stops during fill phase
Repeated E1 or E3 errors alongside E7

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose running from the bottom of the tub up to the pressure sensor. Check for kinks, cracks, or disconnected ends. Reconnect or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Clean the air trap chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Find the small chamber at the base of the tub where the pressure hose connects. Remove it and clean out any detergent residue or debris that may be blocking the air passage.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure sensor. For an analog switch type, blow gently into the port and listen for a click at two or three levels. For an electronic sensor, measure the output voltage or frequency with a multimeter while applying gentle air pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring to control board
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the pressure sensor back to the main control board. Check connectors for corrosion or loose pins. Measure continuity through each wire.

whenToCallPro: If testing the electronic pressure sensor requires specialized equipment or if the control board input circuitry is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E7 water level sensor error fix

faq.q: What does the pressure sensor actually do?
faq.a: It measures the air pressure in a small tube connected to the tub. As water fills the tub, the trapped air is compressed, and the sensor detects the pressure change to determine the water level.

faq.q: Can I bypass the pressure sensor?
faq.a: No. The pressure sensor is a critical safety component that prevents overfilling and flooding. Operating without it is dangerous.

faq.q: Does hard water affect the pressure sensor?
faq.a: Hard water can cause mineral deposits in the air trap chamber at the tub, which can block the pressure signal. Regular descaling helps prevent this.

title: Water Temperature Abnormality
shortDescription: The detected water temperature is outside the expected range for the selected cycle.
fullDescription: Error E8 is triggered when the NTC temperature sensor reports a water temperature that is significantly higher or lower than what is expected at a given point in the cycle. This can indicate a runaway heater, a shorted sensor, or an incorrect sensor reading.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has short-circuited, reading extremely low resistance
NTC temperature sensor has open-circuited, reading infinite resistance
Heater relay on the control board is stuck on, causing overheating
Hot water supply is connected to the cold inlet by mistake

symptoms: E8 error displayed on the control panel
Water excessively hot to the touch during a cold cycle
Steam coming from the machine unexpectedly
Cycle aborts during the heating phase
Clothes damaged by excessive heat

fixSteps.title: Verify plumbing connections
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the cold water supply is connected to the cold inlet (usually marked blue) and the hot supply to the hot inlet (marked red), if applicable. A swapped connection can cause unexpected temperatures.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the NTC sensor and measure its resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 4.7-6 kilo-ohms. If it reads near zero or infinite ohms, replace the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater wires and measure between each heater terminal and the metal tub or heater body. There should be no continuity. If there is continuity, the element has a ground fault and must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater stays on even when the control board should have turned it off, the heater relay on the main PCB may be stuck closed. This requires professional board-level diagnosis or replacement.

whenToCallPro: If you suspect the control board relay is stuck, as this requires professional board-level repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E8 temperature error fix

faq.q: Is E8 dangerous?
faq.a: Potentially yes, if the heater is stuck on and overheating the water. Unplug the machine immediately and do not use it until the fault is resolved.

faq.q: Can the NTC sensor be repaired?
faq.a: No. NTC sensors are inexpensive sealed components and must be replaced as a whole unit if faulty.

faq.q: What temperature is too hot for a washer?
faq.a: Most washers limit water temperature to about 90°C. If the sensor detects temperatures approaching or exceeding this limit, E8 will trigger as a safety measure.

title: Communication or PCB Error
shortDescription: Internal communication between the display board and main control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error E9 indicates a communication failure between the user interface (display) board and the main control board. This can be caused by a loose ribbon cable, a faulty connector, or a failed circuit board. The machine will be unresponsive to user input.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or wiring harness between boards is loose or disconnected
Display (UI) board has malfunctioned
Main control board has malfunctioned
Connector pins are corroded due to moisture ingress

symptoms: E9 error displayed on the control panel
Buttons are unresponsive or erratic
Display shows garbled or random characters
Machine does not start any cycle
Intermittent loss of display

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to allow the control boards to fully reset. Plug it back in and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel of the washer to access the wiring between the display board and main control board. Reseat the ribbon cable or connector at both ends, ensuring firm contact.

fixSteps.title: Inspect connectors for corrosion
fixSteps.instruction: Examine all connector pins for green or white corrosion. Clean corroded pins gently with electrical contact cleaner and a small brush. Ensure the area is dry before reconnecting.

fixSteps.title: Test board voltages
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine powered on, carefully measure the supply voltage at the display board connector. The main board should be providing 5V or 12V DC to the display board. If no voltage is present, the main board may be faulty.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not resolve the issue, professional diagnosis is needed to determine which board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E9 communication PCB error fix

faq.q: Can moisture cause E9?
faq.a: Yes. If moisture or condensation reaches the control board connectors, corrosion can develop over time and disrupt the signal between boards.

faq.q: Which board usually fails — the display or the main board?
faq.a: The display board fails more often because it is exposed to more humidity near the control panel area. However, both boards should be tested.

faq.q: Is it cheaper to replace one board or the whole machine?
faq.a: A single board replacement is usually cost-effective. If both boards need replacement, compare the combined cost against a new machine, especially for units over 7 years old.

title: Slow Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer is draining water too slowly, though not completely blocked.
fullDescription: Error E21 indicates the washer is able to drain water, but the drainage rate is slower than the control board expects. This is typically caused by a partially blocked drain filter, a restricted drain hose, or a weakening drain pump. Unlike E2, some water is being removed, but not fast enough.

causes.cause: Drain filter is partially clogged with lint and small debris
Drain hose has a partial kink or obstruction
Drain pump impeller is partially obstructed or worn
Standpipe or household plumbing has a partial blockage

symptoms: E21 error displayed on the control panel
Wash cycles take longer than usual
Some water remains in the drum after draining
Drain pump runs for extended periods
Occasional musty smell from stagnant water

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the filter access panel at the bottom front, drain residual water into a dish, and remove the filter. Clean it under running water, removing all lint, hair, and debris. Also clean inside the filter housing.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the standpipe or sink. Run water through it to check for partial blockages. Flex the hose along its length to dislodge any trapped debris.

fixSteps.title: Check the standpipe
fixSteps.instruction: Pour a kettle of hot water down the standpipe or sink drain to clear any buildup. If drainage is slow at the standpipe itself, the household plumbing may need clearing.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump and check the impeller for wear or partial obstruction. A weak pump may drain slowly even when the path is clear. If the pump is more than 5 years old and the impeller blades are worn, consider replacement.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter and hose does not resolve the slow drain, the pump may need professional inspection or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E21 slow drain error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E2 and E21?
faq.a: E2 means the washer cannot drain at all within the timeout period. E21 means it is draining, but too slowly. E21 is typically a partial blockage rather than a complete one.

faq.q: Can fabric softener cause slow draining?
faq.a: Excessive fabric softener can leave a waxy residue that accumulates in the drain filter and pump housing over time, contributing to slow drainage.

faq.q: Should I use drain cleaner in the washer?
faq.a: Do not pour chemical drain cleaner into the washing machine. Use the drain filter cleaning procedure instead. Drain cleaner can damage rubber seals and the pump.

title: Drain Pump Relay Error
shortDescription: The control board has detected a fault in the drain pump relay circuit.
fullDescription: Error E23 indicates the main control board has detected that the drain pump relay is not switching correctly. The relay may be stuck open (pump never runs) or stuck closed (pump runs continuously). This is typically a control board issue rather than a pump problem.

causes.cause: Drain pump relay on the main control board has failed
Drain pump is drawing excessive current and damaging the relay
Wiring between the control board and drain pump is short-circuited
Control board solder joints around the relay are cracked

symptoms: E23 error displayed on the control panel
Drain pump runs continuously even when the machine is idle
Drain pump never activates during drain phase
Burning smell from the control board area
Machine will not complete any wash cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the control board. If the relay was stuck due to a transient condition, this may clear it.

fixSteps.title: Check drain pump current draw
fixSteps.instruction: Connect the washer to a watt meter or use a clamp meter on the pump wires during a drain cycle. If the pump draws significantly more than its rated wattage (typically 25-40W), it may be seizing and overloading the relay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the wiring harness from the main board to the drain pump for any signs of damaged insulation, pinched wires, or bare conductors that could cause a short circuit.

fixSteps.title: Replace or repair the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay is confirmed faulty, the main control board needs replacement. In some cases, a technician can resolder or replace just the relay on the board to save cost.

whenToCallPro: E23 usually requires control board replacement or repair, which is best handled by a qualified technician.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E23 drain pump relay error fix

faq.q: Can a relay be replaced on the control board?
faq.a: Yes, a skilled electronics technician can desolder the failed relay and replace it with an equivalent part, which is cheaper than replacing the entire board.

faq.q: Why does the pump run when the machine is off?
faq.a: If the drain pump relay is stuck closed (welded contacts), the pump will run whenever the machine is plugged in, regardless of the cycle state. Unplug the machine until repaired.

faq.q: Is E23 related to E2?
faq.a: They are related in that both involve the drain pump. E2 is a timeout error when the pump fails to drain water. E23 specifically indicates the relay circuit controlling the pump has a fault.

title: Motor Overcurrent Error
shortDescription: The motor is drawing more electrical current than the control board deems safe.
fullDescription: Error E51 indicates the motor control circuitry has detected that the motor is drawing current above its safe operating limit. This can be caused by a seized bearing, an overloaded drum, a failing motor, or a short in the motor winding. The machine stops as a protective measure.

causes.cause: Drum is heavily overloaded with laundry
Drum bearings are seized or heavily worn
Motor winding has a partial short circuit
Foreign object jammed between drum and tub

symptoms: E51 error displayed on the control panel
Loud grinding noise from drum bearings
Burning smell from motor area
Drum difficult to turn by hand
Machine stops abruptly during wash or spin

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove some items from the drum to bring the load within the rated capacity. Restart the cycle. If E51 persists with a normal load, proceed with further diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Check drum rotation by hand
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine off, try rotating the drum by hand. It should spin smoothly with moderate resistance from the belt. Grinding, scraping, or inability to turn indicates a bearing or obstruction problem.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the drum paddles or lifters (usually held by clips or screws) and check for items like underwire, coins, or buttons that may have fallen between the drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Test motor current draw
fixSteps.instruction: Use a clamp meter on the motor supply wire while running a cycle. Compare the reading to the motor nameplate rating. If current is significantly above the rated value with a normal load, the motor or bearings are faulty.

whenToCallPro: Bearing replacement requires splitting the outer tub and is a complex repair best left to a professional technician.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E51 motor overcurrent error fix

faq.q: How do I know if the bearings are worn?
faq.a: Spin the empty drum by hand. A metallic grinding or rumbling sound, or play (wobble) in the drum shaft, indicates worn bearings.

faq.q: Is bearing replacement worth the cost?
faq.a: For machines less than 5-6 years old, bearing replacement is usually worthwhile. For older machines, compare the repair cost against the price of a new washer.

faq.q: Can an overloaded drum damage the motor?
faq.a: Yes. Consistently overloading the drum forces the motor to draw excessive current, which overheats the windings and dramatically shortens the motor's lifespan.

title: Heater Relay Error
shortDescription: The control board heater relay circuit has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error E61 indicates the main control board has detected a fault with the relay that controls the heating element. The relay may be stuck, or the feedback circuit may be reporting an inconsistency between the commanded and actual relay state. This prevents the heater from being safely controlled.

causes.cause: Heater relay contacts on the control board are welded shut
Heating element has a ground fault causing relay overload
Control board solder joints near the relay have cracked
Wiring between the relay and heater is short-circuited

symptoms: E61 error displayed on the control panel
Heater stays on continuously, overheating water
Heater never activates despite selecting a hot wash
Cycle aborts during heating phase
Slight burning smell from the control board area

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to fully reset the control board. Plug it in and try a hot wash cycle. If E61 recurs, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater wires and measure between each heater terminal and the metal tub. There should be no continuity (infinite resistance). If you get a reading, the heater has a ground fault and must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Check heater wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring between the main control board and the heating element for any damage, bare wires, or signs of overheating. Replace any damaged sections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay is confirmed faulty (stuck open or stuck closed), the main control board needs replacement. A technician may be able to replace just the relay component.

whenToCallPro: E61 typically requires control board repair or replacement, which should be handled by a qualified appliance technician.
videoSearchQuery: Toshiba washer E61 heater relay error fix

faq.q: Is E61 a safety hazard?
faq.a: If the heater relay is stuck on, the heater will operate uncontrolled, which can overheat the water. Unplug the machine immediately until repaired.

faq.q: Can I run cold washes with E61?
faq.a: It depends on the specific failure. If the relay is stuck open, the heater simply will not work and cold washes should function. If stuck closed, the heater runs uncontrolled on any cycle, which is unsafe.

faq.q: How much does a relay replacement cost vs. a whole board?
faq.a: A technician who can replace just the relay may charge 40-80 for labor. A full replacement board costs 80-200 for the part alone. Board-level repair is more economical when available.
